UserAccount.title: UserAccount
UserAccount.description: Represents a user's account, which can be either personal or business.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserAccount entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User entity. (Relationship: User 1:N UserAccount)

accountName.description: Name of the account (personal name or business name).

createdDate.description: Date and time when the account was created.

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Represents an invoice created by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Invoice entity.

userAccountId.description: Reference to the UserAccount that created the invoice. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:N Invoice)

clientId.description: Reference to the Client the invoice was sent to. (Relationship: Client 1:N Invoice)

issueDate.description: Date when the invoice was issued.

dueDate.description: Date when the invoice is due.

PaymentLink.title: PaymentLink
PaymentLink.description: Represents a payment link created by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PaymentLink entity.

userAccountId.description: Reference to the UserAccount that created the payment link. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:N PaymentLink)

amount.description: Amount to be paid through the link.

createdDate.description: Date and time when the payment link was created.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a transaction made by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

userAccountId.description: Reference to the UserAccount involved in the transaction. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:N Transaction)

transactionDate.description: Date and time of the transaction.

invoiceId.description: Reference to the Invoice associated with the transaction (if applicable). (Relationship: Invoice 1:N Transaction)

paymentLinkId.description: Reference to the PaymentLink associated with the transaction (if applicable). (Relationship: PaymentLink 1:N Transaction)

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a client associated with a user's account.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

userAccountId.description: Reference to the UserAccount that the client is associated with. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:N Client)

Wallet.title: Wallet
Wallet.description: Represents a wallet associated with a user account, storing balance information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Wallet entity.

userAccountId.description: Reference to the UserAccount that owns the wallet. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:1 Wallet)

balanceTotal.description: Total balance ever received for the account.

balanceAvailable.description: Available balance that can be withdrawn.

balancePending.description: Pending balance that is not yet available for withdrawal.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the TabEdge platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

signUpDate.description: Date and time when the user signed up.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Contains basic information like email, name, and sign-up date.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: userAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user's account.

params.description: The unique identifier for the user's account.

params.name: walletId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user's wallet.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to provide a secure and scalable solution for TabEdge Pay, focusing on authorization independence and clear data ownership. The structure uses path-based ownership for user-specific data and denormalization to avoid complex security rules that rely on `get()` calls. This approach ensures atomic operations and easier debugging.

**Authorization Independence:** The core strategy is to embed authorization data directly within the documents where it's needed, avoiding hierarchical lookups. For example, data related to a user account (invoices, payment links, transactions) is stored under the `/users/{userId}/accounts/{accountId}` path. This ensures that access control can be determined directly from the document being accessed without needing to query parent documents.

**QAPs (Rules Are Not Filters):** To support secure list operations, the structure avoids mixing data with different access requirements in the same collection. Private user account data is stored under the user's document, while potentially public data (if any) would be stored separately with appropriate security rules.  The membership map model is not required here, as the user ID in the path establishes ownership.

**Structure Highlights:**
*   **Users:** The base collection for user profiles.
*   **UserAccounts:** Each user can have multiple accounts (personal/business), stored as subcollections under the user.
*   **Wallet:** The wallet is a subcollection of accounts, ensuring that only the user can access it.
*   **Invoices, PaymentLinks, Transactions, Clients:** These are all stored as subcollections under the specific user account, clearly associating them with the account.

This structure allows for straightforward security rules based on `request.auth.uid` and the document path, eliminating the need for complex `get()` calls and ensuring a robust and debuggable authorization system.
